title: SDL ServiceManifest
description: The bounded container for a software service description. One manifest per repo. Declares what this service is, what it owns, and what interfaces it exposes and consumes.

dl.notes: ServiceManifest is the top-level Container in SDL. It groups all Operations (Units) within a single service boundary.

dlVersion.description: The SDL spec version this file conforms to. Used by validators to fetch the correct schema version.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this service. Should be stable — used as a reference key by entry-points, dependencies, and synthesis tools.

label.description: Human-readable display name for this service.

description.description: One paragraph describing what this service owns and why it exists. Write for a senior engineer reading it cold.

domain.description: The business domain this service belongs to. Aligns with DDD bounded contexts.

responsibilities.description: Plain-language list of what this service is responsible for. Design intent, not API docs.

technology.description: Technology stack. Informational only — does not affect validation.

exposes.description: The interfaces this service exposes to the outside world. Written in protocol-prefixed notation. These become candidates for inter-Container Connects in a parent-level synthesis.

consumes.description: External interfaces this service depends on. Informational at this level — see dependencies.sdl.json for detail.

contracts.description: Links to external contract definitions this service owns. SDL describes; contracts define precisely.

ref.description: Path or URL to the contract file.

stats.description: Auto-generated summary counts. Populated by tooling, not hand-authored.

meta.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for project-specific metadata.
